compiled_cfg_gate: test_dump_jtbl_structure asserts an m_jtbl block exists at GLBOPT1 with a readable mcases_t (>=4 case->target pairs) -- i.e. the dense small keys (0..4) actually compiled to a JUMP TABLE, not a jz-chain. This IS the L6 cfg-validation gate.
jtbl_emission: clang -O0 mingw emits m_jtbl for >=4 DENSE small keys (case 0..4). Large sparse 32-bit states (the other lab fixtures) stay a jz if-chain -- density+count, not magnitude, drives table lowering.

glbopt1_structure.note: handlers blk3..7 each goto the re-dispatch join blk9 -> jtbl blk2; the entry blk1 gotos blk2 directly. So old_target is PER-WRITER (entry old=2, handlers old=9), NOT a single dispatcher head.

extractor: _jtbl_routing reads tail.r.c (mop_c -> mcases_t): for each target index, cases.targets[j] + cases.values[j] -> {case_value: target_serial}. No state-constant floor / guessing -- the case->target map is built into the jump table. state slot = the jtbl index operand (tail.l), SSA {ver} suffix stripped to match writers' version-free dest.
live_render: PASS via optblock_t (GLBOPT1, return change-count). The m_jtbl switch is fully DRAINED into a straight-line chain: g_hexrays_lab_sink = token+0x11; ...^0x22; ...-0x33; ...+0x44; ...^0x55; return. No switch, no jtbl, no state var, mba.verify() clean.
general_rule: Deobfuscation DRAINS a switch dispatcher, it does NOT insert one. Reading mcases_t gives case->handler directly; redirecting each state-writer to its routed handler (per-writer old_target = the writer's own single successor) collapses the jump table to the linear handler chain it hid. d810's existing m_jtbl ops (NormalizeNWayDispatcherExit / CanonicalizeJumpTableCaseOverlap) only normalize an existing table -- none INSERT one.
test: tests/system/runtime/hexrays/test_insert_unflatten_mini.py::TestInsertUnflattenJtbl (test_dump_jtbl_structure + test_jtbl_renders_linear_optblock); 14 passed / 1 xfailed across the lab file.
